name: CYK Parsing of Context-Free Grammars
description: The CYK algorithm decides membership in a context-free language in cubic time using dynamic programming: the grammar is put in Chomsky normal form, a table is filled for substrings of length one, then for increasing spans every split point is tried to derive nonterminals; the string is accepted if the start symbol spans the whole input.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["String + CFG"]
    N2["Convert to CNF"]
    N3["Init DP table"]
    N4["Fill length-1 cells"]
    N5["Increase span length"]
    N6["Try all split points"]
    N7{"Production matches?"}
    N8["Add nonterminal to cell"]
    N9{"More spans?"}
    N10["Next span"]
    N11{"Start symbol on top?"}
    N12["Accept / reject"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N7 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|yes| N10
    N10 -->|loop| N5
    N9 -->|no| N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Recognition and parsing of context-free languages in time n^3
Introduction to Automata Theory, Languages, and Computation (3rd ed.)
sources.authors: Hopcroft, J. E.; Motwani, R.; Ullman, J. D.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.
